meaning: n.杂乱,混乱,无秩序状态vt.扰乱,使失调,使紊乱
examples: The FDA has approved a new treatment for a rare blood disorder.
I was relieved to find out that my problems were due to a genuine disorder.
Anyone can develop an eating disorder, but young women are most vulnerable.
Poor nutrition during pregnancy almost triples the rate of antisocial personality disorder in adulthood.
However, in the vast majority of cases of bipolar disorder there would no history of flu.
